title: Anthrax Surveillance and Analysis Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4mqpv","text":"This study employs a systematic review and retrospective analysis to assess anthrax incidence, prevalence, and risk factors in Zimbabwe. Data were sourced from peer-reviewed literature, grey veterinary records from the Central Veterinary Laboratory (CVL), and geospatial analysis using GIS. Anthrax prevalence was estimated using weighted prevalence analysis, with a calculated regional prevalence of 17.33% across Zimbabwe, Kenya, Botswana, and Namibia. Logistic regression modelling was applied to examine climate influences, revealing a positive correlation (OR = 2.41, p \u003c 0.05) between outbreaks and post-drought rainfall events. Spatial analysis identified high-risk zones in Midlands, Mashonaland Central, and Matabeleland North using the Getis-Ord Gi hotspot detection method*. This methodology provides a reproducible framework for anthrax surveillance, incorporating epidemiological, statistical, and GIS-based approaches to inform climate-adaptive risk mapping and targeted intervention strategies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":746,"length":37}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"2mvuj","text":"1. Intended Use and Scope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bh3qg","text":"‱ This protocol is designed for researchers, epidemiologists, veterinarians, and public health officials working on anthrax surveillance and analysis.
‱ It provides step-by-step instructions for data collection, statistical modeling, GIS-based risk mapping, and climate-anthrax correlation analysis.
‱ The methodology is applicable to Zimbabwe and other anthrax-endemic regions with similar ecological and epidemiological conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":32,"length":72},{"style":"bold","offset":165,"length":25},{"style":"bold","offset":195,"length":103},{"style":"bold","offset":335,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":151,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":300,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7l54f","text":"","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5l3vt","text":"2. Experimental Controls and Quality Assurance","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":46}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8lv34","text":"‱ Data Validation: Cross-check anthrax incidence data from peer-reviewed sources and grey literature reports to ensure consistency.
‱ GIS Accuracy: Validate hotspot mapping results by comparing them with historical outbreak records.
‱ Statistical Robustness: Test logistic regression model sensitivity using bootstrap resampling.
‱ Reproducibility Check: Ensure that different analysts using the same dataset obtain the same spatial and statistical results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":16},{"style":"bold","offset":59,"length":21},{"style":"bold","offset":85,"length":23},{"style":"bold","offset":134,"length":13},{"style":"bold","offset":157,"length":23},{"style":"bold","offset":204,"length":27},{"style":"bold","offset":235,"length":23},{"style":"bold","offset":308,"length":20},{"style":"bold","offset":332,"length":22},{"style":"bold","offset":409,"length":47}],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":1},{"key":4,"offset":132,"length":1},{"key":5,"offset":233,"length":1},{"key":6,"offset":330,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6902f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dc492","text":"3. Key Considerations Before Running the Protocol","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":49}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4v74","text":"‱ Ethical and Data Access Considerations","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":38}],"entityRanges":[{"key":7,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cfgue","text":"If using laboratory-confirmed anthrax data, ensure institutional review board (IRB) approvals where required.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":51,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9m5s2","text":"Some datasets, such as national disease surveillance reports, may require permission from government agencies before Common Pitfalls to Avoid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[{"key":8,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6m27q","text":"GIS Mapping Errors: Ensure that spatial coordinate systems are correctly set before overlaying anthrax case data onto base maps.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":32,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7f5k9","text":"Statistical Model Assumptions: Verify that data meets logistic regression assumptions (e.g., no perfect multicollinearity, balanced classes).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30},{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9th5t","text":"Climate Data Handling: Climate raster data may need pre-processing (e.g., resampling to uniform grid resolution) before analysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22},{"style":"bold","offset":52,"length":60}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sn50","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bm99i","text":"4. Troubleshooting & FAQ","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"41n6m","text":"Q1: What if my GIS mapping outputs do not align with known anthrax outbreak locations?
‱ Check that your spatial reference system (SRS) is set correctly (use WGS 84 projection for global datasets).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":86},{"style":"bold","offset":105,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[{"key":9,"offset":87,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"brsoq","text":"Q2: How do I validate my statistical results?
‱ Run bootstrap resampling to confirm logistic regression output consistency.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":45},{"style":"bold","offset":52,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[{"key":10,"offset":46,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ato56","text":"Q3: How do I modify this protocol for another region?
‱ Replace Zimbabwe-specific datasets with your local anthrax case records, rainfall/temperature data, and land-use maps.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":53},{"style":"bold","offset":64,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[{"key":11,"offset":54,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7jljs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mcck","text":"5. Protocols.io, DOI: XXXX-XXXX."","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":26},{"style":"italic","offset":29,"length":141}],"entityRanges":[{"key":12,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7u0kr","text":"‱ How to Acknowledge Data Sources:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[{"key":13,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1i7gv","text":"If using SILAB laboratory data, cite Zimbabwe Central Veterinary Laboratory (CVL).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":37,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58eqe","text":"Climate data must be cited according to ERA5 Reanalysis Data (Copernicus Climate Change Service).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":40,"length":56}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9m3n7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bgjjd","text":"6. steps: Type: Systematic review and retrospective analysis. Study Period: 2013–2024. Scope: Evaluation of anthrax prevalence in livestock, wildlife, and human cases . Primary Outcomes: Identification of anthrax hotspots, climate-related outbreak patterns, and policy intervention areas.
Data Sources: Peer-reviewed publications, grey literature from veterinary databases.

Software & Tools: Statistical analysis: R, SPSS, Python (SciPy, StatsModels) . GIS mapping: QGIS, ArcGIS . Climate data: ERA5 Reanalysis, Zimbabwe Meteorological Services data . Disease surveillance: SILAB Veterinary Laboratory Database
Literature Review
Databases Used: PubMed, Web of Science, JSTOR, AGRICOLA, HINDARI, Google Scholar, Scopus.
Search Terms: "Anthrax", "Zimbabwe", "livestock anthrax", "wildlife anthrax", "zoonotic diseases", "Bacillus anthracis", "transboundary disease".
Inclusion Criteria: Peer-reviewed studies on anthrax in Zimbabwe (2013–2024). Reports on livestock, wildlife, and human anthrax cases. English language publications.
Grey Data Collection from the Central Veterinary Laboratory (CVL)
Exclusion Criteria: Studies without outbreak data. Environmental studies lacking direct epidemiology insights.
Source: Zimbabwe’s National Veterinary Laboratory (SILAB database) .
Data Extracted: Anthrax-positive cases in livestock, wildlife, and humans. Sample types: swabs, spleen, soil, tissue. Geographic and temporal distribution of cases.
Prevalence Calculation: Prevalence = (Positive Cases / Total Cases) x 100
Weighted Prevalence Analysis
To compare anthrax trends in Zimbabwe, Kenya, Botswana, Namibia, a weighted prevalence was calculated: Weighted Prevalence = å (Prevalence x Weight)
Weighting Factor: (Sample size of each region) / (Total sample size across regions).
Confidence Interval Estimation
Wilson Score Interval for 95% CI: Where: - X = Positive cases. - N = Total cases. - z = 1.96 (for 95% CI).
Logistic Regression for Climate-Anthrax Relationship
Model: Dependent Variable: Anthrax outbreak occurrence (Binary: 1 = outbreak, 0 = no outbreak). Independent Variables: Rainfall (mm), Temperature (°C), Drought Index (SPI)
GIS-Based Spatial Analysis Software: QGIS, R
Hotspot Detection: Getis-Ord Gi* statistic:
creator: Tawanda A Chari
